example: Marley was the sole partner and co-proprietor of the counting house of Scrooge and Marley.
Marley was as dead as a doorknail.
ja: ドアの釘（非常に死んでいることの比喩）

example: Scrooge was as solitary as an oyster.
He spoke out shrewdly in his grating voice.
No beggars implored him to bestow a trifle.
On Christmas Eve, a biting foggy day, Scrooge sat at his desk.
ja: 刺すような、厳しい寒さの

example: His clerk was in a dismal little cell, trying to warm himself.
Two portly gentlemen entered, soliciting donations for the poor.
If they would rather die, they had better do it and decrease the surplus population.
Scrooge went home to his gloomy old chambers.
He saw, instead of the door knocker, the face of Jacob Marley.
ja: ドアノッカー、ノックするもの

example: A clanking dreadfully disturbing noise came from the cellar.
ja: ガチャガチャと鳴る音

example: It was Jacob Marley, dragging an immense heavy chain forged of cash boxes.
I am doomed to wander the earth, witnessing human scenes.
ja: 運命づけられた、破滅する運命の

example: You will be haunted by three spirits.
The air was filled with mournful, restless fountains.
ja: 悲しげな、哀愁を帯びた

example: Filled with a great, though still skeptical, dread.
Are you the spirit whose coming was foretold to me?
ja: 予言された、予告された

example: They passed instantaneously through the walls.
His old boarding school, cheerless and deserted.
He was an apprentice in a bustling warehouse.
Fezywig presided over a grand Christmas party.
ja: 司会を務める、主宰する

example: You praise him as if he were a benefactor of mankind.
Another idol has displaced me, a golden one.
She dissolved their engagement.
The ghost remained impassive.
He extinguished the light emanating from its head.
Enthroned upon this mountain of food sat a giant spirit.
It bore a glowing torch shaped like a horn of plenty.
The spirit sprinkled incense from its torch upon them.
Tiny Tim was a cripple.
ja: 身体障害者、足の不自由な人

example: He bore a little crutch and wore iron frames upon his limbs.
Scrooge hung his head in penitence.
A mighty voice called his name.
Two pitiful, wretched children emerged.
The boy is ignorance.
I see that written, which is doom, unless the writing be erased.
A solemn dark phantom shrouded in a deep black garment came gliding in.
Phantom shrouded in a deep black garment.
He could see nothing but the folds of its black attire.
The rich man was so despised that no one would attend his funeral.
They were at an infamous rag and bone shop.
On the miserable, uncurdened bed lay a lonely body.
ja: 負担のない、重荷のない

example: They had a day's grace to gather the payment, a small reprieve from ruin.
ja: 執行猶予、一時的な救済

example: The family was quietly mourning.
ja: 喪に服すること、悲しみ

example: Scrooge fell to his knees before the specter.
He reached out to clutch the spirit's hand.
The phantom's hood and dress dwindled down into a bedpost.
He encountered one of the portly gentlemen who had solicited charity.
A great many back payments are included in it.
Bob was aghast, believing his master had gone mad.
Some people laughed at his transformation.
Tiny Tim uttered a favourite saying.
